mn17:1.2: At one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn17:11-14.1: He dwells relying on a certain town …
mn17:15-18.1: He dwells relying on a certain city …
mn17:19-22.1: He dwells relying on a certain district …
mn17:2.1: “*Bhikkhus*, I will teach you an exposition on the forest tract.
mn17:2.2: Listen to that, attend closely, I will speak.”
mn17:23.1: He dwells relying on a certain person.
mn17:23.2: While he is dwelling relying on that person, *sati* that is not established does not become established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] does not become settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted do not become exhausted, and he does not attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that he had not yet attained.
mn17:23.3: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained with difficulty.
mn17:23.5: ‘I am dwelling relying on this person. While I am dwelling relying on this person, *sati* that is not established does not become established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] does not become settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted do not become exhausted, and I do not attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.
mn17:23.6: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained with difficulty.’
mn17:23.7: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, that *bhikkhu* should leave that person, whether by night or by day, without asking permission; he should not keep following him.
mn17:24.1: But here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* dwells relying on a certain person.
mn17:24.2: While he is dwelling relying on that person, *sati* that is not established does not become established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] does not become settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted do not become exhausted, and he does not attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that he had not yet attained.
mn17:24.3: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained without much difficulty.
mn17:24.5: ‘I am dwelling relying on this person. While I am dwelling relying on this person, *sati* that is not established does not become established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] does not become settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted do not become exhausted, and I do not attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.
mn17:24.6: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained without much difficulty.
mn17:24.7: It was not for the sake of robes that I went forth from the household life into homelessness, nor for the sake of almsfood … nor for the sake of lodgings … nor for the sake of medicinal requisites for the sick that I went forth from the household life into homelessness.
mn17:24.8: And yet, while I am dwelling relying on this person, *sati* that is not established does not become established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] does not become settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted do not become exhausted, and I do not attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.’
mn17:24.9: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, that *bhikkhu* should leave that person without hesitation, having taken leave; he should not keep following him.
mn17:25.1: But here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* dwells relying on a certain person.
mn17:25.2: While he is dwelling relying on that person, *sati* that is not established becomes established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted become exhausted, and he attains the unsurpassed security from bondage that he had not yet attained.
mn17:25.3: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained with difficulty.
mn17:25.5: ‘I am dwelling relying on this person. While I am dwelling relying on this person, *sati* that is not established becomes established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted become exhausted, and I attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.
mn17:25.6: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained with difficulty.
mn17:25.7: It was not for the sake of robes that I went forth from the household life into homelessness, nor for the sake of almsfood … nor for the sake of lodgings … nor for the sake of medicinal requisites for the sick that I went forth from the household life into homelessness.
mn17:25.8: And yet, while I am dwelling relying on this person, *sati* that is not established becomes established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted become exhausted, and I attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.’
mn17:25.9: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, that *bhikkhu* should keep following that person without hesitation; he should not depart.
mn17:26.1: But here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* dwells relying on a certain person.
mn17:26.10: The seventh, the Forest Tract, is finished.
mn17:26.2: While he is dwelling relying on that person, *sati* that is not established becomes established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted become exhausted, and he attains the unsurpassed security from bondage that he had not yet attained.
mn17:26.3: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained without much difficulty.
mn17:26.5: ‘I am dwelling relying on this person. While I am dwelling relying on this person, *sati* that is not established becomes established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted become exhausted, and I attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.
mn17:26.6: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained without much difficulty.’
mn17:26.7: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, that *bhikkhu* should keep following that person even for life; he should not depart, even if he is being sent away.”
mn17:26.9: Satisfied, those *bhikkhus* approved of what the *Bhagavā* said.
mn17:3.1: “Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* dwells relying on a certain forest tract.
mn17:3.2: While he is dwelling relying on that forest tract, *sati* that is not established does not become established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] does not become settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted do not become exhausted, and he does not attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that he had not yet attained.
mn17:3.3: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained with difficulty.
mn17:3.5: ‘I am dwelling relying on this forest tract. While I am dwelling relying on this forest tract, *sati* that is not established does not become established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] does not become settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted do not become exhausted, and I do not attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.
mn17:3.6: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained with difficulty.’
mn17:3.7: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, that *bhikkhu* should depart from that forest tract, whether by night or by day; he should not stay there.
mn17:4.1: But here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* dwells relying on a certain forest tract.
mn17:4.2: While he is dwelling relying on that forest tract, *sati* that is not established does not become established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] does not become settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted do not become exhausted, and he does not attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that he had not yet attained.
mn17:4.3: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained without much difficulty.
mn17:4.5: ‘I am dwelling relying on this forest tract. While I am dwelling relying on this forest tract, *sati* that is not established does not become established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] does not become settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted do not become exhausted, and I do not attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.
mn17:4.6: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained without much difficulty.
mn17:4.7: It was not for the sake of robes that I went forth from the household life into homelessness, nor for the sake of almsfood … nor for the sake of lodgings … nor for the sake of medicinal requisites for the sick that I went forth from the household life into homelessness.
mn17:4.8: And yet, while I am dwelling relying on this forest tract, *sati* that is not established does not become established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] does not become settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted do not become exhausted, and I do not attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.’
mn17:4.9: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, that *bhikkhu* should depart from that forest tract without hesitation; he should not stay there.
mn17:5.1: But here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* dwells relying on a certain forest tract.
mn17:5.2: While he is dwelling relying on that forest tract, *sati* that is not established becomes established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted become exhausted, and he attains the unsurpassed security from bondage that he had not yet attained.
mn17:5.3: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained with difficulty. Therefore, *bhikkhus*, that *bhikkhu* should reflect thus:
mn17:5.4: ‘I am dwelling relying on this forest tract.
mn17:5.5: While I am dwelling relying on this forest tract, *sati* that is not established becomes established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted become exhausted, and I attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.
mn17:5.6: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained with difficulty.
mn17:5.7: It was not for the sake of robes that I went forth from the household life into homelessness, nor for the sake of almsfood … nor for the sake of lodgings … nor for the sake of medicinal requisites for the sick that I went forth from the household life into homelessness.
mn17:5.8: And yet, while I am dwelling relying on this forest tract, *sati* that is not established becomes established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted become exhausted, and I attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.’
mn17:5.9: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, that *bhikkhu* should stay in that forest tract without hesitation; he should not depart.

While I am dwelling relying on this forest tract, *sati* that is not established becomes established, and mind [*cittaṁ*] that is not settled in *samādhi* [*asamāhitañca*] becomes settled in *samādhi* [*samādhiyati*], and *āsavas* that are not yet exhausted become exhausted, and I attain the unsurpassed security from bondage that I had not yet attained.
mn17:6.6: And those requisites of life that are to be obtained by one gone forth—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicinal requisites for the sick—are obtained without much difficulty.’
mn17:6.7: Therefore, *bhikkhus*, that *bhikkhu* should stay in that forest tract even for life; he should not depart.
